title: Introduction to Probability
subtitle: A Modern Introduction Built on Harvard's Celebrated Stat 110 Course
author: Joseph K. Blitzstein and Jessica Hwang
publisher: Chapman and Hall / CRC Press (Taylor & Francis)
subjects: Binomial, Poisson, Normal, Exponential, Gamma, Beta
Law of large numbers and central limit theorem
themes: Probability as a language for reasoning under uncertainty
Story-based intuition for distributions and conditioning
Connecting discrete and continuous probability through the Uniform
Simulation and computation in R as a tool for building intuition
Modern applications: PageRank, MCMC, genetics, information theory
awards: Developed from Harvard's Stat 110, one of the most popular undergraduate courses at the university
Standard reference for probability courses at Harvard, Stanford, MIT, and hundreds of other institutions
Stat 110 YouTube lectures have accumulated millions of views
Widely adopted as the textbook for the edX 'Introduction to Probability' online course
excerpt: Introduction to Probability by Joseph K. Blitzstein and Jessica Hwang is the modern, story-driven introduction to probability built from Harvard's celebrated Stat 110 lectures. With lucid prose, deep conceptual intuition, and an unmatched collection of 'story proofs,' it has become the most widely used probability textbook of its generation — equally valuable as a first course for undergraduates and as a reference for data scientists, statisticians, and machine-learning practitioners.
